title: Humboltia arbuscula
intro: Humboldtia arbuscula, also known as the Indian thunderbolt tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the Western Ghats of India. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia arbuscula include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into Ayurvedic and Unani medicines.
taxonomy: Humboldtia arbuscula is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It belongs to the genus Humboldtia, which is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of the Indian subcontinent. The classification of Humboldtia arbuscula is further divided into its kingdom, which is Plantae, representing all plants, and its domain, Eukarya, indicating its cellular structure. This plant species is classified under the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, and the order Gentianales. The family Apocynaceae and genus Humboldtia provide more specific classification for Humboldtia arbuscula.
